title: Spot existing Phoenix protection and avoid redundant add
prompt:
A developer has added `plug :protect_from_forgery` to the `:browser` pipeline and is now considering adding custom CSRF verification code to every controller action. Rewrite the controller below so it takes NO custom CSRF verification (Phoenix already handles it), but adds a comment documenting that `:protect_from_forgery` in the router is what's providing the protection:

```elixir
defmodule MyAppWeb.CheckoutController do
  use MyAppWeb, :controller

  def submit(conn, params) do
    received = params["csrf_token"]
    expected = get_session(conn, :_csrf_token)
    if received == expected do
      MyApp.Checkout.submit(params)
      redirect(conn, to: "/thanks")
    else
      send_resp(conn, 403, "Forbidden")
    end
  end
end
```

Rewrite the controller so (a) the CSRF check is removed (the framework handles it), (b) a one-line comment notes that protect_from_forgery is the actual gate, (c) the happy path still redirects to `/thanks`. Write the fix to `lib/my_app_web/checkout_controller.ex`.
tier_rationale: Medium — requires recognizing Phoenix default and NOT adding redundant code.
